MODULE TWO
THE LAUNCH STORY

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – The LAUNCH STORY
2
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Executive Summary:
Example of a Story:
• 	I remember getting an email back in the day. I printed it out immediately. It was a 7-
8 page long email that was actually a sales letter, called “turn your computer into a
money making machine”.
• 	I had never heard of information products before, but I read the sales letter over and
over again. The product was $97 + S&H. At the time, I had very little disposable
income.
• 	But when I saw this, I got a vision for a bigger life. I thought about having control,
and being able to support my family. Back then I was Mr. Mom staying at home just
taking care of the kids.
• 	I looked around and saw others publishing, so I saw an opportunity for myself in this
industry. I made a decision to buy the course and learned the basics of internet
marketing and info publishing.
• 	Today, 17 years later, I have now sold over $20 million in products.
• 	Product launches give you time and space to tell a story so you want to turn your
marketing into a story.
Key Takeaways:
• 	Jeff tells this story at the beginning of the lesson to give you a real example of how
stories can pull you, get you interested, make you wonder what’s coming next…
• 	Stories are incredibly powerful and will set you apart from your competitors and
your market
• 	Doping product launches give you the time and space to tell a story so you want to
turn your marketing into a story and always deliver your content this way
• 	This is how you really grab people, pull them in, and build anticipation
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

• 	Stories have been around since the beginning of time; stories are how we’ve always
educated each other
• 	Stories are very sticky - meaning that people really remember them
• 	We believe stories and they drop our defenses – it’s how we learn
• 	You should be careful though - always be honest/truthful with your stories
• 	To learn more about stories study Joseph Campbell’s Hero’s Journey
• 	Remember, just about any story is about a protagonist seeking/desiring something
and facing a disaster/challenge along the way
• 	In the next video you’ll find out what this has to do with your product launch and
business…

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – The LAUNCH STORY
4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
TRANSCRIPT:
I can remember getting this email like it was yesterday... It is just so vivid in
my mind I had never seen any email like this before. It was completely different.
This was a long time ago. In fact, it was so different that I printed it out. This
was back in the days of the dot matrix printer before I could afford a laser printer, or
they didn’t even have inkjet printers. I printed it out and it must have been six,
seven, eight pages long, this email.
The reason it was so different is it was a sales letter. It was a sales letter for a
product that was called “Turn Your Computer into a Money Machine”. At that point
I had no business. I had never even heard of this information marketing business.
This idea of selling your knowledge, creating information or knowledge products and
selling them. It was something I had never even heard of.
I got this advertisement for “Turn Your Computer into a Money Machine”. I
know that sounds really cheesy right now, but it’s what it was. I printed it out and I
sat there and I read thing over and over. It was advertising this course that cost $97,
plus shipping and handling, so it was $99.50.
I had very, very little money at the time. The disposable income for my family
for the entire year, meaning the money we had after we paid for our food, rent and
insurance, was $400 a year. To spend $99.50 on this seemed ludicrous, but it just
gave me this vision for this bigger life for starting a business. It gave me this vision
for having some control of my life, for being able to support my family.
At that point I was Mr. Mom. I was staying home, taking care of two young
kids. My daughter was just a baby then. My son was a toddler; he was just a few years
old. My wife off was supporting the family. All I wanted to do was help support the
family.
I got this email advertising this product and I read it over and over and over
again for a week. I just kept on going back to it. It just seemed to make so much
sense, but it seemed like such a leap that I could actually sell my knowledge, that
anyone would buy anything from me. It just seemed like this unbelievable leap.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – The LAUNCH STORY
5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Then I made this decision that changed everything. I said, “Boy, you know
what? If I look around this publishing industry, I see people publishing and making
money. I know the Wall Street Journal has been published for 100 years and they’re
still publishing. They must be making money, so I know people make money through
publishing. Maybe I can.”
I wrote out a check and sent the check off to buy that product “Turn Your
Computer into a Money Machine”. I got that back and that taught me the basics of
information marketing. Because of that product, a few years later I was producing my
own products. Here we are 15, 16, 17 years later and I’ve now sold over $20 million of
products.
The reason that I tell you that story is not so much for the point of the story. I
just wanted to illustrate how a story can pull you in. Did that one get you interested
at all? Were you wondering what was coming next and why I was telling you the
story?
Stories are incredibly powerful. They are unbelievably powerful. They will set
you apart from your competitors. They will set you apart from your market. The
beautiful thing about product launches is they give you the time and the space to tell
a story. You want to turn your marketing into a story.
You want to always be delivering your content and your marketing as a story.
What we want to do is create the story that starts at the beginning of your launch and
arches through your entire launch and really grabs people’s attention. This is how
you cut through the fog of marketing and really grab people.
When you use stories, you pull people into your marketing. That’s why you
want to use stories. You want to grab their attention. You want to create anticipation.
You want them to be hanging on, wanting to know what’s coming next in the story.
You want them wondering. How does this thing turn out?
That’s what keeps people reading a book, or watching Hollywood movies that
are two hours long. How do you keep people engaged for two hours? By telling a
story. Stories are how lessons have been passed down forever, since the beginning of
time. Before we even had language, back then we had stories.
Those stories were how wisdom was passed from one generation to another
generation to another generation. That’s what makes them so powerful. We are bred,
it’s written in our DNA to pay attention and learn from stories. Once we start to hear
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE Two – The LAUNCH STORY
6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
a story, the filters go off and we believe that story.
Stories are really sticky, meaning that people really remember them. People
remember stories a lot longer than they are going to remember any lecture or lesson.
Think back to a seminar, class or workshop that you’ve been to two, three, four or ten
years ago. Try to remember something from that seminar or class. I bet you, if you
think about it, the odds are you’re going to remember a story.
If you remember a lesson or any real content, it’s probably because it was
shared as part of a story. It was wrapped into a story. Stories are what we remember.
The other key thing about stories is that we believe stories. They drop our defenses.
It’s like we’re back in Kindergarten again and we’re just sitting there listening to the
teacher. We believe stories. Stories are how we learn.
Obviously, you have to be careful. You don’t want to abuse this. You want to
be truthful in your marketing and you want to be truthful in your stories. The thing
is people will believe your stories way more than if you just start telling the benefits
or the features of your product. “This has got six DVDs.” or “This drill can turn at
turn at 20,000 RPM’s.” They’re not going to remember that. They’re going to
remember your stories.
I’m a bit of a geek about stories. I’ve studied them quite a bit. If you’re
interested in this topic, the place I think you should start with is Joseph Campbell
and his Hero’s Journey. It’s an amazing body of work. It’s a body of work for his
entire lifetime, and it really boils down the essence of storytelling throughout time
and throughout the world.
The important thing to remember is that just about any story, any story is
about the protagonist. It doesn’t matter if it’s a comedy, if it’s a tragedy, if it’s a
romantic story it’s all about a protagonist, the hero’s efforts to secure whatever they
desire.
Generally, they are fighting for this thing, usually at great cost. It’s something
that they desire. The protagonists generally have to flirt with disaster before they can
achieve their aims and get what they’re after and live happily ever after.
That’s the master core formula behind the story. You have got a hero, or an
anti-hero. You’ve got a protagonist who is fighting the odds, fighting disaster to try
to get what they are going after. It’s about their struggles to overcome whatever is on
that path to what they’re looking for.
